activities.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate empathy for other people’s experiences nationally and globally
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of literature and its functions
suggested_methods: Concept mapping: Guide students through explaining the concept of literature
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the functions of literature
Story telling: Guide students through narrating a story and writing down notes on what they perceive as possible literary qualities and functions of that story
activities.assessment_criteria: The concept of literature and its functions are well explained
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts, pictures and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate empathy for other people’s experiences nationally and globally
activities.learning_activity: (b) Explain the concepts of oral and written literature
suggested_methods: Project activity: Guide students through collecting literary materials found and shared in their community
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing how the literary materials they have collected are stored and shared
Questions and answers: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the meanings and characteristics of oral and written literature
Skills Lab: Guide students in manageable groups through classifying the collected oral and written literary materials in terms of their genres and sub-genres
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts of oral and written literature are clearly explained

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate empathy for other people’s experiences nationally and globally
activities.learning_activity: (c) Relate people’s life experiences with folktales, songs, riddles, proverbs, fables and idioms from the Tanzanian context
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through identifying various people’s life experiences evident in their immediate surroundings
Project activity: Guide students through collecting folktales, songs, riddles, proverbs, fables and idioms found and shared in their community
Engaging with oral materials: Organise students in manageable groups to analyse people’s life experiences reflected in oral materials
activities.assessment_criteria: People’s life experiences with folktales, songs, riddles, proverbs, fables and idioms from the Tanzanian contexts are well related

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate empathy for other people’s experiences nationally and globally
activities.learning_activity: (d) Reflect on the characters’ actions, attitudes and experiences in selected novels
suggested_methods: Close reading: Guide students through studying characters’ personalities (gender, status, deeds, choices, appearance, feelings and judgement) in a selected novel
Discussion: Guide students through discussing characters’ actions, attitudes and experiences in a selected novel
activities.assessment_criteria: Characters actions, attitudes and experiences in selected novels are well reflected on

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Appreciate multiculturalism and develop self-consciousness
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concepts of multiculturalism and self-consciousness in the context of literature
suggested_methods: Role play: Guide students through role playing various personalities of interest to them
Simulation: Guide students through simulating various personalities of interest to them
Discussion: Guide students through discussing the concepts of multiculturalism and self-consciousness in the context of literature
Close reading: Guide students through identifying and discussing varying beliefs, values, symbols, customs, language and practices in a selected text
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts of multiculturalism and self-consciousness in the context of literature are well explained
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Appreciate multiculturalism and develop self-consciousness
activities.learning_activity: (b) Reflect on one’s cultural values against those depicted in selected novels
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups through sharing information on beliefs, customs, values and taboos they associate with
Questions and answers: Guide students through discussing and reflecting on their cultural values against those depicted in a selected novel
activities.assessment_criteria: One’s cultural values against those depicted in selected novels are well reflected on

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Appreciate multiculturalism and develop self-consciousness
activities.learning_activity: (c) Assess the development of the protagonist and antagonist characters in selected novels
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing protagonist and antagonist characters’ personalities, backstories, goals, strengths and weaknesses
Jigsaw: Guide students through discussing their insights on characters’ development
activities.assessment_criteria: The development of protagonist and antagonist characters in selected novels is clearly assessed

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Reflect values, perspectives and identities, and the impact of their beliefs and actions on society
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concepts of values, perspectives and identities
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide students through discussing attitudes, thoughts, religious and cultural values familiar to them
Role play: Guide students through role playing the impact of their actions and beliefs in a society
activities.assessment_criteria: Concepts of values, perspectives and identities are well explained
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Develop awareness of and empathy with national and global literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Reflect values, perspectives and identities, and the impact of their beliefs and actions on society
activities.learning_activity: (b) Reflect on the characters’ perspectives, and values against one’s own
suggested_methods: Engagement with a text: Guide students individually through identifying the main and minor characters’ perspectives and values
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing characters’ perspectives and values and relate them with their own
activities.assessment_criteria: The characters’ perspectives, and values against one’s own are clearly reflected on

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Appreciate the use of literary devices in the works of art
activities.learning_activity: (a) Analyse narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected literary texts
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups through brainstorming about what makes the literary works they know interesting.
Close reading: Guide students through analysing the narrative techniques and stylistic devices used in a selected literary text
Jigsaw: Guide students through discussing the narrative techniques and stylistic devices used in a selected literary text
activities.assessment_criteria: Narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected literary texts are clearly analysed
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Appreciate the use of literary devices in the works of art
activities.learning_activity: (b) Explain the significance of narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected novels
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the significance of narrative techniques and stylistic devices in a selected novel
activities.assessment_criteria: Significance of narrative techniques and stylistic devices in selected novels are well explained

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Appreciate the use of literary devices in the works of art
activities.learning_activity: (c) Discuss literary devices which evoke emotions in selected novels
suggested_methods: Concept mapping: Guide students through explaining the concept of literary devices
Engagement with texts: Guide students through discussing literary devices which evoke emotions in a selected novel

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Discuss the roles of observation, analysis, inference, communication and problem-solving in literature
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students through brainstorming various methods of problem-solving
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the roles of observation, analysis, inference, communication and problem-solving in literature
Engagement with texts: Guide students through suggesting solutions to problems depicted in a selected text
activities.assessment_criteria: Roles of observation, analysis, inference, communication and problem-solving in literature are well discussed
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Assess the relationship among characters in selected novels
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students through brainstorming how people are variously related in the community
Close reading: Guide students through assessing the characters’ relationship in a selected text
activities.assessment_criteria: The relationship among characters in selected novels is well assessed

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (c) Discuss the lessons derived from the choices and decisions of characters in selected novels
suggested_methods: Think-Ink-Share: Guide students through sharing various lessons they have learned following their decisions and choices made in various life contexts
Engagement with a text: Guide students through discussing characters’ choices and decisions
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing possible lessons resulting from characters’ actions and decisions
activities.assessment_criteria: Lessons derived from the choices and decisions of characters in selected novels are well discussed

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (d) Identify major themes in the selected novels
suggested_methods: Scaffold reading: Guide students through four reading stages (setting the scene, assigning tasks, asking questions and giving feedback) to grasp details such as how the story starts and ends, major and minor events, and their likes and dislikes about the story
activities.assessment_criteria: Major issues in the selected novels are correctly identified

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Appreciate different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (e) Analyse themes such as corruption and bribery in selected novels
suggested_methods: Thematic triad: Guide students through identifying thematic ideas found in a selected novel and evaluate them to get themes
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing themes conveyed in a selected text
activities.assessment_criteria: Themes such as corruption and bribery in selected novels are correctly analysed

activities.main_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
activities.learning_activity: (a) Justify the actions and context of characters in relation to themes in selected novels
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide student through brainstorming how issues in their society are related to people’s actions and context
Close reading: Guide students through studying characters’ actions, language and context in a selected novel
Discussion: Guide students through discussing how the setting, circumstances, language and characters’ actions, contribute to the development of themes
activities.assessment_criteria: The actions and context of characters in relation to themes in selected novels are correctly justified
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
activities.learning_activity: (b) Discuss the relationship between the title and messages in selected novels
suggested_methods: Project activity: Engage students in manageable groups to collect several stories and news titles and identify which and why some titles grab attention, predict content and establish tone of the story

Follow-up on project activity: Guide students through reflecting on the mind-set and skills they used to study the titles they collected to identify the relationship between titles of selected novels and messages they are likely to convey
activities.assessment_criteria: The relationship between the title and messages in selected novels is well discussed

activities.main_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Justify interpretations based on evidence and logic
activities.learning_activity: (c) Discuss the relevance of the novel to one’s society
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups through brainstorming several socio-political and economic issues in their community
Discussion: Guide students through discussing and relating issues they have identified to those depicted in a selected novel
activities.assessment_criteria: The relevance of the content to one’s society is well discussed

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Discuss forms of poetry and figurative language
suggested_methods: Engagement with texts: Guide students in manageable groups through identifying sound patterns, verse and stanza’s structures, style and language of several types of poems (haiku, ballad, sonnet and lyrics)
Discussion: Engage students through discussing forms of poetry and mwanipulation of language
Resource person: Invite a guest speaker to present the forms of poetry and figurative language
activities.assessment_criteria: Forms of poetry and figurative language are well discussed
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Analyse themes portrayed in selected poems
suggested_methods: Recitation: Guide students through reciting selected poems in class and identifying themes portrayed
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing themes portrayed in selected poems
activities.assessment_criteria: Themes portrayed in selected poems are well analysed

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (c) Discuss lessons derived from selected poems
suggested_methods: Jig saw: Guide students in manageable groups through studying repetitions and persona’s actions and speeches focusing on how they suggest varying lessons
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing lessons derived from selected poems
activities.assessment_criteria: Lessons derived from selected poems are well discussed

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (d) Apply literary skills to compose a poem
suggested_methods: Project activity: Guide students in manageable groups through thinking about a topic, and form, writing the first line, developing ideas and devices, writing the closing line and editing the poem
Shared composition: Collaborate with students to compose a poem
Collaborative composition: Guide students in pairs or groups through composing a poem
Independent composition: Guide students through composing a poem on a topic of their choice
activities.assessment_criteria: Literary skills for composing a poem are correctly applied

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concepts of antithesis, anticlimax, paradox, irony, unreliable narrator and suspense in literary works
suggested_methods: Story telling: Guide students through telling stories and identifying exciting, apprehensive and uncertain conditions, disappointing ends, self-contradicting statements, and deliberate misleads
Discussion: Guide students through discussing antithesis, anticlimax, paradox, irony, unreliable narrator and suspense in literary works
Explanation: Guide students in manageable groups through applying the acquired knowledge to explain antithesis, anticlimax, paradox, irony, unreliable narrator and suspense
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts of antithesis, anticlimax, paradox, irony, unreliable narrator and suspense are well explained
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
activities.learning_activity: (b) Discuss the relationship between the title and the content of selected plays
suggested_methods: Think-Ink-Share: Guide students through sharing the relationship between people’s physical appearance and behaviour
Close reading: Guide students in manageable groups through relating the title and the central idea of selected plays
Discussion: Guide students through discussion how the tittle relates to the content of selected plays
activities.assessment_criteria: The relationship between the title and content of selected plays is well discussed

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
activities.learning_activity: (c) Discuss the effect of ironical titles in selected plays
suggested_methods: Think-ink-share: Guide students through sharing statements/scenarios/events reflecting something said but means the opposite; the opposite of what is expected happens; and the audience knows something that characters do not
Knowledge-based approach: Guide students through using gained knowledge from the previous activity to discuss the effectiveness of ironical titles of selected plays
Discussion: Guide students through discussing how the title relates to the content of the selected plays
activities.assessment_criteria: The effect of ironical titles in selected plays are well discussed

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.3 Create imaginative and original literary works
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concepts of creativity and visualization in literary composition
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide students through discussing features of newspapers, fictional and non-fictional works
Knowledge-based approach: Guide students through applying the knowledge gained from the discussion to explain the concepts of creativity and visualisation/imagination
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts of creativity and visualization in literary composition are correctly explained
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts, ICT tools and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.3 Create imaginative and original literary works
activities.learning_activity: (b) Make a plan for composing a poem (decide what to write about, determine the best format, explore words, rhymes, and rhythm)
suggested_methods: Project activity: Task students to research about several issues within their immediate surroundings and compose a poem about them
Shared planning: Collaborate with students to make a plan (outline, select words, write, edit, revise and organise the idea into a full poem) for composing a poem
activities.assessment_criteria: A plan for composing a poem is clearly made

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.3 Create imaginative and original literary works
activities.learning_activity: (c) Apply writing skills to compose a poem
suggested_methods: Independent composition: Guide each individual student through composing a poem on a topic of their choice
activities.assessment_criteria: Writing skills for composing a poem are correctly applied

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 3.3 Create imaginative and original literary works
activities.learning_activity: (d) Apply ICT tools and digital platforms in writing and publishing a poem
suggested_methods: Skills lab: Task students to use ICT tools and writing skills to compose poems
Publishing: Guide students through publishing their poems on available digital platforms
activities.assessment_criteria: ICT tools and digital platforms are appropriately

activities.main_competence: 1.0  Appreciate   different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (a)  Analyse themes depicted in selected plays
suggested_methods: Engagement with a text: Guide students in manageable groups through reading, analysing and discussing the depicted themes in a selected play
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing themes depicted in selected plays
activities.assessment_criteria: Themes  depicted in selected plays are well analysed
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 1.0  Appreciate   different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (b)  Analyse conflicts portrayed in selected plays
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing characters relationships, tensions and misunderstandings in selected plays
activities.assessment_criteria: Conflicts portrayed in selected plays are well analysed

activities.main_competence: 1.0  Appreciate   different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (c)  Relate personal experiences to themes in selected plays
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups through brainstorming personal life experiences
Discussion: Guide students through relating personal life experiences to themes portrayed in selected plays
activities.assessment_criteria: Personal experiences are well related to themes in selected plays

activities.main_competence: 1.0  Appreciate   different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (d)  Discuss the  motivations of characters’ choices and decisions in selected plays
suggested_methods: Engagement with a text: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing intrinsic and extrinsic reasons for character’s behaviors, choices and decisions in selected plays
activities.assessment_criteria: Motivations of characters’ choices and decisions in selected plays are well discussed

activities.main_competence: 1.0  Appreciate   different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (e)  Suggest solutions to challenges depicted in selected plays
suggested_methods: Dramatisation: Guide students through staging some scenes portraying challenges in a selected play

Discussion: Guide students through discussing and suggesting solutions to the depicted challenges by applying principles of resolution (affiliate, empathize, engage, own, self-restrain and build trust)
activities.assessment_criteria: Solutions to challenges depicted in selected plays are clearly suggested

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (a)  Explain the concepts of character, plot, setting, stage direction and spectacle in a play
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the concepts of character, plot, setting, stage direction and spectacle
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts of character, plot, setting, stage direction and spectacle are well explained
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (b)  Analyse stages of plot development
suggested_methods: Project activity: Task students to analyse stages of plot development a play of their choice (exposition, rising action, climax, falling action and resolution)
Exploration: Guide students in manageable groups through exploring stages of plot development
activities.assessment_criteria: Stages of plot development are correctly analysed

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
activities.learning_activity: (c)  Apply literary skills to compose a two-acts play
suggested_methods: Collaborative composition: Guide students in pairs or groups through composing a play
activities.assessment_criteria: Literary skills for composing a two-acts play are correctly applied

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
activities.learning_activity: (a)  Discuss dramatic and situational irony in selected plays
suggested_methods: Engagement with texts: Guide students through analysing congruities between what happens and what is expected; what is known to audience and what is known to characters
Discussion: Guide students through discussing dramatic and situational irony in selected plays
Explanation: Guide students through explaining the concepts of dramatic and situational ironies
activities.assessment_criteria: Dramatic and situational irony in selected plays are well discussed

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
activities.learning_activity: (b)  Discuss the effectiveness of suspense in selected plays
suggested_methods: Engagement with texts: Guide students in manageable groups through analysing how conflict, pacing, red herrings, atmosphere and high stakes are used in selected plays
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing how suspense controls the story line in selected plays
activities.assessment_criteria: The effectiveness of suspense in selected plays is well discussed

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
activities.learning_activity: (c)  Explain how antithesis creates ambiguity in selected plays
suggested_methods: Engagement with texts: Guide students through identifying exciting, apprehensive and uncertain conditions; disappointing ends; self-contradicting statements; and deliberate misleads in selected plays
Discussion: Guide students through discussing how antithesis creates ambiguity in selected plays
activities.assessment_criteria: Creation of ambiguity through antithesis in selected plays is well explained

activities.main_competence: 1.3    Create imaginative and original literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
activities.learning_activity: (a) Make a plan for composing a play (plot, setting, characterisation and stage direction)
suggested_methods: Shared planning: Collaborate with students to make a plan for composing a play
Collaborative planning: Guide students in pairs or manageable groups through making a plan for composing a play
activities.assessment_criteria: A plan for composing a play is correctly made
suggested_resources: Selected literary texts, ICT tools and audio/audio-visual materials

activities.main_competence: 1.3    Create imaginative and original literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
activities.learning_activity: (b)  Apply literary skills to compose a two-acts play
suggested_methods: Independent composition: Guide individual students through composing a two-acts play
activities.assessment_criteria: Literary skills for composing a two-acts play are correctly applied

activities.main_competence: 1.3    Create imaginative and original literary works
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
activities.learning_activity: (c)  Apply ICT tools and digital platforms in writing and publishing a play
suggested_methods: Skills lab: Guide students through composing a play using ICT tools
Publishing: Guide students independently or in manageable groups through publishing their plays on available digital platforms
activities.assessment_criteria: ICT tools and digital platforms are appropriately applied in writing and publishing a play
